digest.top_line: mRCAT-III: pCR 61.0% vs 28.6% (P<0.0001) for node-sparing SCRT + CAPOX + tislelizumab in pMMR LARC, though node-sparing and added IO are confounded.
digest.tldr: Two trials probe RT subtraction. In ES-SCLC, adding concurrent thoracic RT to 1L chemoIO missed (OS 10.0 vs 11.8mo, HR 1.14): the CREST-era consolidation signal doesn't carry into the IO era. In LARC, dropping elective nodal RT alongside added tislelizumab doubled pCR, but the two changes are confounded and locoregional control is unreported. Thoracic and lower-GI carried the day.

sites.intro: A clean negative for concurrent thoracic RT in ES-SCLC: the consolidation-RT benefit seen in CREST's chemo-only era doesn't transfer to 1L chemoimmunotherapy.

studies.name: LBA8005: Concurrent TRT + Chemoimmunotherapy in ES-SCLC
studies.tldr: OS 10.0 vs 11.8mo, HR 1.14 (0.84-1.56), p=0.40: concurrent thoracic RT added no survival benefit to 1L chemoimmunotherapy in ES-SCLC.
details: 🔍 Randomized phase III, concurrent TRT n=115 vs chemoIO alone n=113, 1° endpoint OS
🔍 TRT 30 Gy/10 fx given concurrently (start day 21-28, cycle 2), not as post-chemo consolidation
💊 Backbone: durvalumab 1500mg + carboplatin AUC5 + etoposide x4, then durvalumab maintenance to PD
🔍 PCI 25-30 Gy optional to responders
📊 Even in the subgroups most likely to benefit (chemoIO completers, no brain/liver mets), TRT showed no OS advantage
🔗 vs CREST (Slotman, Lancet 2015): same 30Gy/10fx but consolidative in the chemo-only era; that late-OS signal does not carry into the IO era here
⚠️ Concurrent timing and an unselected (not response-enriched) population differ from CREST's design; not a clean replication
⚠️ Toxicity was a key secondary; no AE numbers reported in source

verdict.rationale: Randomized phase III, OS primary endpoint null (numerically worse with TRT); contradicts CREST-era rationale for adding thoracic RT in the chemoimmunotherapy setting.
verdict.audience: Extensive-stage SCLC, treatment-naive, ECOG 0-1, stable brain mets allowed

studies.significance: Concurrent thoracic RT (30 Gy/10 fx) adds nothing to 1L chemoimmunotherapy in ES-SCLC, and the subgroups where CREST's late benefit would be expected (chemoIO completers, no brain/liver mets) were null too. But this tested concurrent-during-induction RT in an unselected population, not CREST's consolidative-to-responders design, so consolidative TRT stays an open question.
open_questions: Does consolidative (not concurrent) thoracic RT still benefit ES-SCLC responders in the IO era?
Any role for TRT in oligometastatic or low-burden ES-SCLC subsets?

sites.intro: A practical concurrency map for RT + systemic therapy with RNI, gating which agents continue, pause, or hold during breast/chest-wall RT.

studies.name: RT + Systemic Therapy Concurrency in Breast Cancer (Speers)
studies.tldr: Concurrency map for breast/CW + RNI: continue endocrine, trastuzumab/pertuzumab, T-DM1; caution T-DXd and CDK4/6i; hold cytotoxics, capecitabine, veliparib/talazoparib.

details.text: 🔍 Default outside protocols: PK washout (5×t½) → RT → resume; t½ gates hold duration
subdetails: Talazoparib 5t½ ~19 days (long PK tail complicates brief holds)
Olaparib 5t½ ~3 days; pembrolizumab 5t½ ~110 days (holding by PK rarely practical)

details.text: 📊 Traffic-light: concurrent systemic therapy with breast/CW + RNI (ASCO Educ Book 2026, Table 5)

rows: Sequence/hold for high lung-dose or active pulmonary disease
Hold large fields; concurrent only in protocol
Limited fields; concurrent only in protocol
Adjuvant paradigm sequential; resume after RT

details.text: 📊 ADC ILD signal: T-DXd vs T-DM1 (the dominant RT-relevant toxicity)

details: 📊 T-DXd + RT timing (DESTINY-Breast05): ILD 10.7% sequential vs 9.6% concurrent, no effect
📊 COMBART: 40 pts concurrent RT/SRT, acute toxicity 20%
💊 KEYNOTE-522 post-hoc (1,174 pts, 715 received RT): concurrent pembro + RT well tolerated, numerically fewer G3-5 AEs vs sequential
💊 P-RAD (TBCRC-053): preop RT 0/9/24 Gy + pembro; 24 Gy arm → significant T-cell infiltration at 2 wks (RT as biologic stress test)
⚠️ Veliparib: avoid concurrency, dose-limiting moist desquamation + fibrosis (TBCRC 024)
⚠️ Olaparib: RT must complete 2-12 wks before; concurrent only in limited fields (RadioPARP signal)
studies.significance: The actionable read is T-DXd: DESTINY-Breast05's RT-timing data (ILD 10.7% sequential vs 9.6% concurrent, no effect) licenses concurrent T-DXd + RT with monitoring rather than a blanket hold, except for high lung-dose plans or baseline ILD. PK washout, not reflexive holding, governs the rest.
open_questions: Prospective concurrent-RT safety data for CDK4/6 inhibitors
Optimal T-DXd RT timing in high lung-dose plans
Concurrent olaparib + RT safety beyond RadioPARP

related_trials.brief_summary: This phase II trial studies how well radiation therapy with or without olaparib works in treating patients with inflammatory breast cancer. Radiation therapy uses high energy x-rays to kill tumor cells and shrink tumors. Olaparib is an inhibitor of PARP, an enzyme that helps repair deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) when it becomes damaged. Blocking PARP may help keep cancer cells from repairing their damaged DNA, causing them to die. PARP inhibitors are a type of targeted therapy. It is not yet known…

sites.intro: A pCR doubling with PD-1 in typically IO-refractory pMMR rectal, but node-sparing and added tislelizumab are confounded and locoregional endpoints are unreported.

studies.name: mRCAT-III
studies.tldr: pCR 61.0% vs 28.6% (P<0.0001) for node-sparing SCRT + CAPOX + tislelizumab vs conventional SCRT + CAPOX in pMMR LARC.
details: 🔍 Phase 3 open-label RCT, N=154 (77/arm), 17 China sites; primary pCR by blinded independent central review
🔍 RT: short-course 5Gy×5d (25Gy/5fx) both arms; experimental spared elective nodal RT (tumor bed only, no draining nodes)
💊 Experimental added tislelizumab 200mg IV to the CAPOX backbone; control got CAPOX alone
⚠️ Confounded: experimental arm both spared nodal RT AND added tislelizumab, so pCR gain can't be attributed to node-sparing alone
⚠️ pCR can't detect nodal failure, the risk omitting elective nodal RT creates; locoregional control and EFS unreported
🔗 Control pCR 28.6% matches SCRT + consolidation-chemo TNT benchmarks (e.g. RAPIDO); increment reflects added IO plus node-sparing
⚠️ Fewer severe GI AEs reported in experimental arm; no effect size reported in source
🔍 MSS/pMMR rectal is typically IO-refractory; a pCR doubling with PD-1 here is the provocative signal, pending confirmation

verdict.rationale: Experimental arm confounds node-sparing RT with added tislelizumab, so the RT contribution can't be isolated; pCR surrogate, locoregional control and survival unreported.
verdict.audience: pMMR/MSS LARC, cT3-4N0/+, tumor ≤10cm from anal verge

studies.significance: The RT read is target volume: the experimental arm both spared elective nodal RT (tumor bed only) and added tislelizumab, so the pCR doubling can't isolate node-sparing's contribution. pCR is also blind to nodal failure, the very risk nodal omission creates, so EFS (pending), not pCR, gates whether to drop elective nodal coverage with IO.
open_questions: Is the pCR gain from node-sparing RT or added tislelizumab?
Does omitting elective nodal RT compromise locoregional control?
Will the pCR benefit translate to organ preservation, EFS, OS?

Tumor-draining lymph nodes (TDLNs) are critical sites for anti-tumor immune activation, but radiation-induced damage and fibrosis may impair lymphatic drainage and immune responses. Takeaway Points
• This trial provides evidence that combining node-sparing radiotherapy with
tislelizumab and chemotherapy is an effective treatment strategy for patients
with MMR LARC.
• The experimental arm achieved a remarkable doubling of the pCR rate
(61.0% vs 28.6%, P<0.001), meeting the primary endpoint.
• This innovative approach not only improves tumor response but also reduces
the incidence of severe gastrointestinal adverse events, indicating an
improved tolerability profile.

KEY TOXICITY SIGNALS
Dermatitis may be underreported (Corbin)
Pneumonitis small signal - maintain vigilance
CNS radionecrosis with SRS - the "do not ignore" signal
USE CAUTION WITH
large lung volumes • IMN coverage • bolus • reconstruction •
CNS SRS
T-DX trastuzumab deruxtecan; t/½ = 6 days, 5t% = 30 days
REASONABLE with caution - monitor closely
DOMINANT TOXICITY - ILD
PI (5.4 mg/kg): ILD ~12%; fatal ~ 0.9%
DESTINY-Breast05: ILD 9.6% T-DXd vs 1.6% T-DM1
RT timing (T-Dd arm): 10.7% seq vs 9.6% concurrent - no effect
COMBART: 40 pts concurrent RT/SRT; acute tox 20%
SEQUENCE OR HOLD FOR
high lung-dose plans • baseline ILD • thoracic/lung RT •
symptomatic pulmonary disease

CLINICAL ONCOLOGY
ASCO® KNOWLEDGE CONQUERS
LANECK

Immunotherapy + Radiation
KEYNOTE-522 normalized concurrency - P-RAD shows RT is immunomodulatory
14
PK REALITY
Pembrolizumab
t½ = 22 days
5 t½ ~ 110 days
→ Holding by PK rarely practical — risk mitigation shifts
to volume, dose constraints, monitoring
KEYNOTE-522
Pembrolizumab + RT - from sequential to concurrent
V1 Original - restart pembro ≥ 2 wk post-RT
V2 Amended - concurrent pembro + RT permitted
Post-hoc: 1,174 patients • 715 received RT • concurrent pembro + RT well tolerated;
Numerically fewer G3-5 AEs and Immune AEs in concurrent group vs. sequential group;
Highest rate in Pembre alone and no RT arm (maybe RT is immunosuppressive?)
pneumonitis vigilance maintained
McArthur et al. CancerRes2023;83(5_Supplement)PD3-01
P-RAD (TBCRC-053) - RADIATION AS A BIOLOGIC STRESS TEST
Preoperative RT (0 / 9 / 24 Gy) + pembrolizumab in HR+/HER2- disease
24 Gy + pembro
Statistically significant f in T-cell infiltration at 2
weeks
Tertiary lymphoid structures
Induced
- microenvironment converted toward
immune-active state
Implication
Short-course preop RT can prime the immune
system - foundation for RADAR / ART
2026 ASCO
ANNUAL
MEETING

AMERICAN SOCIETY OF
CLINICAL
ONCOLOGY
ASCO• KNOWLEDGE CONQUERS
CANCER

15
PARPi, Capecitabine, and CDK4/6i + Radiation
Where caution is non-negotiable - and where to hold
Olaparib
½ = 15 hr, 5t½ = 3 days
OlympiA, RadioPARP, S1706, OlympiAD
RT must complete 2-12 wks BEFORE olaparib
Sequential paradigm in early-stage;
RadioPARP suggests concurrent safety
Tutt et al., NEJM 2021;384:2394-2405
Loap et al., JROBP, 2025 Nov 1: 123(3):726-731
Veliparib
½ = 5.5 hr, 5t½ = 1 day
TBCRC 024
Severe acute + late toxicity with concurrent RT
AVOID concurrency - dose-limiting moist
desquamation, fibrosis
Jagsi et al., J Clin Oncol 2018; 36(13): 1317-1322
Talazoparib
t½ = 90 hr; 5t½ = 19 days
PK tail
Long t½ complicates brief holds
Favors sequential strategy in early-stage
Capecitabine
t½ = ~45 min; 5t½ = 4 hrs
CREATE-X. Phase I/ Concurrent Trial Woodward et al.
Adjuvant paradigm is sequential, not concurrent
AVOID concurrent breast/CW RT outside
protocol; resume after RT
Masuda et al. N Engl J Med. 2017;376(22):2147-2159.
Woodward et al. JROBP. 2017;99(4):777-783.
Abemaciclib / Ribociclib
t½ = 24-55 hr; 5t = 5-11 days
NCT05996107
Limited prospective data; most evidence
retrospective
Hold during RT for large fields;
concurrent feasible in trial setting
Palbociclib
t½ = 28.8 hr 5t½ = 6 days
5 t½ = 6 days
Marrow / GI exposure increases with field size
Practical to hold around RT; resume
promptly after
When in doubt, hold. PK-based washout → RT → resume — the safer default outside protocols

ASCO• KNOWLEDGE CONQUERS

16
Putting It Together: A Traffic-Light Summary
Concurrent systemic therapy with breast / chest wall + RNI
V CONTINUE
Concurrent with RT
Endocrine therapy
Minimal radiosensitization
Trastuzumab ‡ Pertuzumab
Generally safe; modern heart-sparing
! CAUTION
Plan- / setting-dependent
T-DXd
Concurrent reasonable with monitoring;
sequence/hold for high lung-dose or active
pulmonary disease
CDK4/6 inhibitors
Usually hold for large fields; concurrent only in
selected/protocol settings
X HOLD / SEQUENCE
Outside dedicated protocol
Cytotoxic chemotherapy
Anthracyclines, taxanes, platinum -
sequence, do not give concurrently
Capecitabine / PARPI
(veliparib/talazoparib)
Adjuvant paradigm non-concurrent, veliparib +
RT shows severe acute/late tox
T-DM1
Continue during locoregional RT; dermatitis &
pneumonitis vigilance; caution with CNS SRS
Immunotherapy (Pembro)
Generally tolerable; pneumonitis vigilance +
immune-toxicity workflows
Olaparib
Reasonable with limited fields; concurrent only
in selected/protocol settings
mTOR / PISK agents
Mucosal, skin, metabolic, inflammatory toxicity
risks; ESMO-ESTRO advises caution
